resources.name: Assimil — "French With Ease"
resources.why: The gold standard for self-study. Its method front-loads natural, high-frequency dialogue and a daily 20-30 min rhythm that matches a 2-month sprint perfectly. You absorb grammar through repeated exposure instead of memorising rules — exactly the Pareto approach.

resources.name: Coffee Break French (podcast)
resources.why: Bite-sized lessons taught in English by a patient Scottish teacher and a learner who asks the questions you'd ask. Perfect for the listening half of each session and for commute-time review. Free, structured from absolute zero upward.

resources.name: Anki + a frequency deck (e.g. "5000 French words")
resources.why: Spaced repetition is the single highest-leverage habit for vocabulary. A frequency-ordered deck means every card you learn is one you'll actually hear. 15 minutes a day here compounds faster than any app's gamified vocabulary.

resources.name: InnerFrench (podcast & YouTube)
resources.why: Once you reach A2/B1, Hugo speaks slow, clear French about interesting ideas — comprehensible input that pulls you to fluency. The best bridge from 'studying French' to 'thinking in French'.

resources.name: italki — a native tutor
resources.why: Nothing replaces speaking with a human. Two 30-minute conversation lessons a week forces output, fixes fossilised errors, and builds the confidence apps can't. Cheap, flexible, and the fastest route to actually talking.
